name: Maple Walnut Cake (Gateau a L'erable Et Noix)
recipeIngredient: Eggs, beaten to blend and at room temperature, 2
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Grease two 8- to 9-inch round cake pans.
Using an electric mixer, cream butter and brown sugar until light and fluffy.
Add eggs in a thin stream, beating constantly.
In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and baking soda.
Alternately add dry ingredients and maple syrup with hot water to the butter mixture,
blending until smooth.
Bake for about 30 minutes or until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and allow to cool completely.
Wrap and chill thoroughly.
For praline:
Combine chopped walnuts and maple syrup in a saucepan.
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches 300 degrees F (hard-crack stage) on a candy thermometer.
Remove from heat and stir in the walnuts.
Allow the mixture to cool before spreading onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Cool completely and process into fine pieces.
For whipped cream:
In a large bowl, whip cream and powdered sugar until stiff peaks form.
Add maple extract if desired.
To assemble:
Trim off any cake frosting from the top of each layer.
Place one layer on a serving platter.
Spread a thin layer of whipped cream over the cake.
Sprinkle with praline pieces.
Place the second layer on top, spreading another thin layer of whipped cream and more praline around the sides.
Use the remaining whipped cream to pipe a lattice design on top and a border around the bottom edge.
Press any remaining praline onto the sides and sprinkle over the top.
Serve at room temperature or chilled.
